name: Flower Patch Quilt
description: Colorful, abstract Flower Blocks alternate with simple-to-piece Nine-Patch Blocks to make this quilt topper. The colors are arranged in a somewhat rainbow order, shifting from one corner of the quilt to the opposite corner.

pieces.specialInstructions: Used in Four-Patch unit assembly via strip piecing with Fabric 33
Used in Four-Patch unit assembly via strip piecing with Fabric 30
For stitch-and-flip on corners of 5-inch squares
For stitch-and-flip on corners of 5-inch squares

instructions: Cut (4) 5-inch squares from each of 31 prints for Flower Blocks
Cut (128) squares 3.5 inches for Nine-Patch Blocks
Cut (124) squares 2.75 inches for Flower Blocks
Cut (7) strips 2 inches x 21 inches for Four-Patch Units
Cut (2) side borders 4.5 inches x 81.5 inches
Cut (2) top/bottom borders 4.5 inches x 71.5 inches
Cut (128) squares 3.5 inches for Nine-Patch Blocks
Cut (124) squares 2.75 inches for Flower Blocks
Cut (7) strips 2 inches x 21 inches for Four-Patch Units
Cut (9) strips 2.5 inches x WOF for binding
Cut (2) lengths 41 inches x 99 inches, pieced to fit quilt top with overlap on all sides

assemblySteps: Join Fabric 30 and Fabric 33 strips (2 inches x 21 inches) to make pieced bands. Press seam allowances toward Fabric 30. Make 7 bands. Cut 2-inch-wide segments from the bands for a total of 64 segments. Join 2 segments as shown to make 32 Four-Patch units.

Referring to the Nine-Patch Block diagram, arrange Fabric 29 and Fabric 33 (3.5-inch) squares and a Four-Patch Unit into 3 rows. Join the parts into rows, and then join the rows. Make 32 Nine-Patch Blocks.
From the 10-inch square pack, cut (4) 5-inch squares for each Flower Block from 31 different prints. Note that some fabrics have 2 squares in the pack and are used to make 2 flowers.

For each Flower Block, draw a diagonal line on the wrong side of the Fabric 29 and Fabric 33 (2.75-inch) squares. With right sides together, position a Fabric 33 square on the top left corner of a colored 5-inch square. Stitch on the drawn line. Trim away the excess and press the triangle up. Repeat with a Fabric 29 square on the top right corner. Make 4 Flower Units alike for each block.
Join 4 of the units to make a Flower Block, rotating the units as shown. Make 31 Flower Blocks.

Referring to the Quilt Assembly, arrange the blocks alternately into 9 rows of 7 blocks each. Pay careful attention to the placement of the Flower Blocks. Join the blocks into rows, and then join the rows.
Sew the longer border strips to the sides of the quilt. Join shorter strips to the top and bottom.
Layer the quilt with batting and backing and baste. Quilt in the ditch around borders and patches. Bind to finish the quilt.
